title: MicLength enum allows reserved value 2-7 but only checks >1
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/frame.py:172-175

The MIC length field extraction and validation:

    mic_field = (llsec >> _MIC_LEN_SHIFT) & _MIC_LEN_MASK  # Extracts 3 bits (0-7)
    if mic_field > MicLength.BITS64:  # BITS64 = 1
        raise FrameError(f'reserved MIC-length value: {mic_field}')

This correctly rejects values 2-7 as reserved. However, the MicLength enum only defines BITS32=0 and BITS64=1. If a future spec version adds BITS128=2, this code will start rejecting valid frames.

More concerning: the comment on line 49 says 'bits 2-4' but _MIC_LEN_MASK is 0b111 which is 3 bits, extracting bits 2-4. This is correct but the spec says 'bits 2-4' which is 3 bits (2,3,4). Double-check spec alignment.
close_reason: Updated MicLength validation in python/src/lichen/link/frame.py:189-195 to use enum constructor (try/except ValueError) making enum the source of truth for valid values (0/1); updated docstring. Matches Rust approach. Tests pass for reserved_mic_length and vectors. Commit 50c0d7a60
